rulings.Case Title: ERIN WIDEN VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs (Ccp 664.6)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2026. Line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT STONE MELET, NICOLE MELET, DIVISADERO PLACE HOME OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, KATE KARDOS, THE EIDELMAN FAMILY TRUST Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs (Ccp 664.6) is continued to April 17, 2026. The Court notes that a Non-Party Objection was submitted for filing on March 13, 2026. This is insufficient time before the hearing to review the filing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JASON VISCARRA VS. ELLEN POON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment And/Or Reconsideration Of Order Granting Summary Jdugment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 473(B) And 1008(A)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2026. Line 2.2. PLAINTIFF JASON VISCARRA Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment And/Or Reconsideration Of Order Granting Summary Jdugment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 473(B) And 1008(A) is continued to April 17, 2026. No later than April 1, 2026 moving party to file a supplemental declaration limited to the following: (1) the date of Defendant's deposition; (2) the date the transcript was requested; (3) whether expedited transcript is offered by the court reporter and what the timeline for expedited and regular transcript is; (4) attach a copy of the opposition to the MSJ highlighting the portions referencing the deposition of Defendant; if none exist state "none." (5) attach a copy of any filing in advance of the hearing on the MSJ highlighting the portions seeking a continuance to obtain further discovery, i.e. a certified copy of the deposition transcript if none exist state "none." (6) the date expert Sims was retained; (7) the date expert Sims provided his findings; (8) a copy of the declaration that Plaintiff's counsel was planning to attach but inadvertently did not attach to the opposition; if none exist state "none." (9) attach a copy of the opposition to the MSJ highlighting the portions referencing expert Sims findings; if none exist state "none." (10) attach filed endorsed copies of the declarations of Graham and Sims filed in support of the motion for reconsideration/relief under CCP 473(b) on or about December 31, 2025. No later than April 1, 2026 moving party to provide a supplemental brief limited to citations (no argument) to cases involving CCP 473(b) relief in the context of opposition to MSJ; including both cases where CCP 473(b) was found to be applicable and was found to be inapplicable. No later than April 1, 2026 opposing party may provide a supplemental brief limited to citations (no argument) to cases involving CCP 473(b) relief in the context of opposition to MSJ; including both cases where CCP 473(b) was found to be applicable and was found to be inapplicable. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL HAIM HOFFMAN ET AL VS. DAVID KOSLOWSKI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Appraiser To Determine Fair Market Value Under Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Maya Rhode
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2026. Line 3. PLAINTIFF DANIEL HOFFMAN AN INDIVIDUAL, JENNY HOFFMAN, JESSICA HOFFMAN Motion To Appoint Appraiser To Determine Fair Market Value Under Partition Of Real Property Act; is continued to May 15, 2026 for status re: filing of the appraisal. The Court will issue a notice pursuant to CCP 874.316(e) within 10 days of May 15, 2026 if appraisal is on file by that date. If appraisal is filed more than 8 days before May 15, 2026 the parties must appear ex-parte within 1 business day of such filing to alert the Court of the filing of the appraisal and request an issuance of the Notice per CCP 874.316(e) ("Notice"). Parties to prepare a form of notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRICK & TIMBER, INC. VS. JHON A. GIMENEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2026. Line 9. DEFENDANT YOANNI ALVIAREZ DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer from notice of entry of order. The Complaint states a cause of action and is not uncertain. The specific conduct violating the cited lease provision, a description for how to cure the violation, and the date of termination, are all stated on the face of the 3 Day Notice to Cure or Quit. This matter will be heard in department 505 by the Honorable Michelle Tong at 1:30 p.m. per the order of the presiding judge. =(505/MT) Parties may appear in-person, or via Zoom Video Conference (Meeting ID: 160 4132 4666 Passcode: 872834). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
